description_long: This object is a cluster of modern residential houses arranged in a stepped, terraced formation on a sloped plot. The houses feature dark, gabled roofs and are aligned in parallel rows, each with a small front yard or entry area. The terrain is divided into a green, grassy section and a large, patterned paved area, likely representing a driveway or communal space. Sparse trees and landscaping elements are visible, adding greenery to the otherwise geometric layout. The houses have multiple windows on their facades, and the overall design emphasizes clean lines and modular repetition. The structure appears to be part of a planned urban neighborhood, with a focus on maximizing space on a sloped site.
description: A group of modern, gabled-roof houses arranged in terraced rows on a sloped, landscaped plot with paved communal areas. The design features dark roofs, multiple windows, and minimalistic landscaping.
description_view_0: This view highlights the front facades of the houses, showing the entryways, gabled roofs, and the transition from the green yard to the paved communal area.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stepped arrangement of the houses along the slope is prominent, and the large paved area is clearly visible.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the alignment of the houses in rows, the sloped terrain, and the patterned paving in the foreground.
description_view_3: The rear or side view shows the length of the houses, the terraced layout, and the extent of the paved area, with less emphasis on entryways.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entryways with steps, minimal overhangs, and simple doors; some units have small porches or paved entry pads.
